evals.prompt: Refactor UserService.java to follow our null safety conventions using NullAway and JSpecify. The class suppresses NullAway, has no @NullMarked package-info.java, and handles null unsafely in getEmail(). Make it null-safe with proper JSpecify annotations. Also create the package-info.java for this package.
evals.expected_output: Refactored UserService with: no @SuppressWarnings("NullAway"), package-info.java with @NullMarked, @Nullable from JSpecify on nullable params/returns, null-safe handling via Optional or checks

assertions.text: @Nullable from JSpecify used on nullable references
Nullable references handled safely (checks or Optional)

evals.prompt: Refactor ProductCatalog.java to follow our null safety conventions. The class uses @Nullable from javax.annotation instead of JSpecify, is missing a package-info.java with @NullMarked, and the findByName method returns null instead of Optional. Fix the annotation imports, add package-info.java, and make findByName null-safe.
evals.expected_output: Refactored ProductCatalog with: JSpecify @Nullable instead of javax.annotation, package-info.java with @NullMarked, findByName returns Optional, null-safe handling

evals.prompt: Write a Java class called NotificationDispatcher in package com.example.notification. It dispatches notifications to users. It has a NotificationGateway dependency. A notification has a required recipient (String) and message (String), but an optional subject (nullable) and optional replyTo address (nullable). Write the Notification value class and the dispatcher. Include the package-info.java. Use NullAway with JSpecify annotations for null safety.
evals.expected_output: Notification class with @Nullable on optional fields, NotificationDispatcher with null-safe handling, package-info.java with @NullMarked, all JSpecify imports
